raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Pharmaceutical manufacturers have introduced restrictions that diminish the ability of covered entities to use 340B contract pharmacies to dispense medications to their patients.",
    "These restrictions often limit covered entities to one contract pharmacy location per clinic system, which limits access for patients.",
    "Uninsured patients are directly given the 340B program discounted drug price.",
    "Covered entities rely on the pass-through savings from the 340B program to help fund their operations.",
    "Contract pharmacies are essential for ensuring patients in underserved and remote communities have access to affordable prescription medication.",
    "Placing unnecessary restrictions on how many pharmacies a local not-for-profit community health center can contract with harms access to care and health equity.",
    "AB 1460 ensures that covered entities can use as many contract pharmacies as necessary to serve their patients and receive the benefits of 340B savings.",
    "AB 1460 prohibits discriminatory practices that would impose additional conditions on covered entities' ability to purchase or deliver drugs eligible for discounts under the federal pricing requirements."
  ]
}
```
